dialogueChunks.intro: The shelves are full, {player_name}. For the first time since he's been gone, the pantry looks the way he used to keep it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I spent the morning organizing everything his way. {target_npc} said the house smells like it used to: like someone's home, not someone's absence. {quest_focus} is getting easier. I even cooked his favorite recipe last night. It wasn't as good as his, but close. Close enough. {quest_stakes} is over.
dialogueChunks.outro: You didn't just bring supplies. You brought back a piece of a life I thought was over. Take this, with more gratitude than words can hold.

playerResponses.text: Close enough is a beautiful thing. Take care of yourself.
He'd be proud to see those shelves. I'm sure of it.
A home that smells like home again. That's the best outcome.

dialogueChunks.intro: Come look, {player_name}. Come look at the garden.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The tomatoes are growing. Small and green, but growing. {target_npc} helped me plant the seeds you brought, and we talked about her: not about how she died, but how she lived. {quest_focus} doesn't fill the hole she left. Nothing will. But it keeps her hands in the soil. {quest_stakes} was never about tomatoes: it was about remembering.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave this garden a second chance. That's more than I can ever repay.

playerResponses.text: Her hands in the soil. That makes perfect sense. Be well.
When those tomatoes ripen, share them the way she would have.

dialogueChunks.intro: The delivery arrived, {player_name}. His last delivery, completed at last.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The settlement sent back a letter of thanks. They had no idea someone died trying to bring it the first time. {target_npc} read it aloud at the tavern and the whole room went quiet. Then someone raised a glass to my brother. Strangers who never knew him, drinking to his memory. {quest_focus} is complete. I cooked his favorite meal that night. The taste is coming back to me, slowly.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_action} is done. His journey is complete, and mine can finally begin again. Take this: it's the least his memory deserves.

playerResponses.text: Kindness that outlasts a life is the greatest legacy. He'd be proud.
A glass raised by strangers. That's a fine memorial.
The taste coming back: hold onto that. It means you're healing.
